Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action, Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay All Discovery
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kathleen Harrison, Esq. 760-487-1047 of Herold & Sager for Plaintiff.
David Suchar, Esq. 612-672-8321 of Maslon for Defendant ARUP NORTH AMERICA.
Roey Rahmil, Esq. 415-421-6500 of Shartsis Friese LLP for Defendant ARUP NORTH AMERICA.
James Tenero, Esq. 415-979-0400 of Selman Breitman for Defendant American Zurich.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues overlap issue. Plaintiff argues this is a duty to defend case. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ARUP NORTH AMERICA LTD.'s Motion To Stay Action, Or, In The Alternative, Motion To Stay All Discovery.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant and cross-complainant Arup North America Ltd.'s motion to stay entire action is denied. (See GGIS Insurance Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1493, 1505 [stay of coverage action not warranted where coverage issues can be decided as a matter of law without making factual determinations that would prejudice the insured in the third-party action]. Arup's alternative motion to stay discovery is granted. (See Riddell, Inc. v. Superior Court (2017) 14 Cal.App.5th 755, 764-771; Haskel v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 963, 980.) The Court finds that Zurich's discovery regarding the scope and character of Arup's work on the underlying projects is logically related to issues affecting liability in the underlying actions. (See, e.g., Zurich Special Interrogatories Nos. 3, 6, 7, 9, 10; Requests for Admission Nos. 3, 4, 7, 8, 9.) Therefore, a stay of discovery is appropriate. Zurich fails to show that a confidentiality order would be sufficient to protect Arup's interests. (See Riddell, 14 Cal.App.5th at 768, 770.) Zurich's claim of prejudice rings hollow. National Union correctly notes that Zurich was either aware of the allegations and extrinsic facts it needed to deny coverage at the time of the tender or it was not; it does not need discovery from Arup to defend National Union's motions. (See Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal.4th 287, 295; Haskel, 33 Cal.App.4th at 977.) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. In addition, the order is without prejudice as to any future motion to stay the entire action that any party may file. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
